name: rn-auth
description: React Native authentication patterns for Expo apps. Use when implementing login flows, Google/Apple sign-in, token management, session handling, or debugging auth issues in Expo/React Native.
capabilities: Implement Google OAuth using expo-auth-session with proper redirect handling
Store tokens securely using expo-secure-store instead of AsyncStorage
Create authentication context with session restoration on app launch
Protect routes using Expo Router segments and auth state
Send authenticated API requests with Bearer tokens
Debug common authentication issues like redirect URI mismatch
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only TypeScript/Python code examples. No executable code, scripts, network calls, file system access, environment variable reads, or external commands exist. All 42 static findings are false positives from pattern misidentification. The scanner misidentified library documentation references as security issues.
valueStatement: React Native authentication requires careful handling of OAuth flows, secure token storage, and session management. This skill provides battle-tested patterns for implementing login with Google, token storage with SecureStore, protected routes with Expo Router, and backend integration.
userTitle: Implement React Native authentication in Expo apps
limitations: Contains documentation and code patterns only, no executable implementation
Does not include backend server code beyond Python reference examples
Apple Sign-In patterns are mentioned but not fully detailed
Does not cover biometric authentication or passkey integration

useCases.title: Add Google login to Expo app
useCases.description: Implement OAuth flow with expo-auth-session including proper token storage and session persistence

useCases.title: Build auth-protected mobile API client
useCases.description: Create authenticated API client that automatically attaches tokens and handles 401 responses with refresh

useCases.title: Add route protection to Expo Router
useCases.description: Implement auth gates that redirect users to login when accessing protected screens

promptTemplates.title: Basic Google Sign-In
promptTemplates.scenario: Add Google authentication to Expo app
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement Google sign-in using expo-auth-session in my React Native Expo app. Include proper redirect handling and token storage.

promptTemplates.title: Secure Token Storage
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I securely store and retrieve authentication tokens in React Native using expo-secure-store? Show me the complete implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Protected Routes
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I protect routes in Expo Router so unauthenticated users are redirected to login? Show the auth context and route guard implementation.

promptTemplates.title: API Client with Auth
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an authenticated API client for my Expo app that automatically adds Bearer tokens, handles 401 errors, and attempts token refresh on expiry.

bestPractices: Always call maybeCompleteAuthSession() at module level to handle OAuth redirects properly
Use expo-secure-store for tokens, never AsyncStorage which is not encrypted
Validate tokens with your backend before trusting user data from OAuth providers
Handle 401 responses by attempting token refresh or forcing logout
antiPatterns: Storing tokens in AsyncStorage - it is not encrypted and accessible to any app code
Skipping maybeCompleteAuthSession() - causes silent auth redirect failures
Using iOS client ID for backend token verification - always use web client ID server-side
Not handling different redirect URIs for Expo Go versus standalone builds

faq.question: Does this skill work with Expo Go and standalone builds?
faq.answer: Yes. Use AuthSession.makeRedirectUri() to automatically generate the correct redirect URI for each environment.

faq.question: What React Native versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill targets Expo SDK 48+ with expo-auth-session 6+. Compatible with React Native 0.73 and later.

faq.question: How do I integrate with my existing backend?
faq.answer: Use the authFetch pattern that attaches Bearer tokens to requests. Handle 401 responses with token refresh or logout.

faq.question: Is my authentication data stored securely?
faq.answer: The skill recommends expo-secure-store which uses iOS Keychain and Android Keystore. Never use AsyncStorage for tokens.

faq.question: Why does auth fail silently in my app?
faq.answer: Most common cause is missing WebBrowser.maybeCompleteAuthSession() call at module level. Also check redirect URI matching.

faq.question: How does this compare to Firebase Auth?
faq.answer: This skill shows native OAuth patterns. Firebase Auth provides a higher-level abstraction but requires the Firebase SDK and Google services.
